channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The Author Behind ‘The Social Network’ Has an Asteroid Movie Coming Out
item.description: 'The Last Orbit,' written by Ben Mezrich, will hit the screen directed by 'Fantastic Four' helmer Matt Shakman.

item.title: Nintendo is suing the US government for a refund of Trump’s illegal tariffs
item.description: Nintendo of America is suing the US government over President Trump's tariffs and is demanding a "prompt refund, with interest" of any duties that it has paid, according to a complaint filed in the US Court of International Trade, as reported previously by Aftermath. The Supreme Court ruled last month that Trump's use of the […]

item.title: Nintendo is suing the US government over Trump's tariffs
item.description: Nintendo of America is suing the US government, including the Department of Treasury, Department of Homeland Security and US Customs & Border Protection, over its tariff policy, Aftermath reports. The video game giant already raised prices on the Nintendo Switch in August 2025 in response to “market conditions” but has so far left the price of the newer Switch 2 console unchanged. Nintendo’s lawsuit, filed in the US Court of International Trade, cites a Supreme Court ruling from February that confirmed lower courts’ opinions that the Trump administration’s global tariffs were illegal. Nintend...

item.title: Microsoft: Anthropic Claude remains available to customers except the Defense Department
item.description: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft products.

item.title: The best deals on MacBooks right now
item.description: It’s 2026, and that means Apple sells MacBooks equipped with not just its capable M-series processors but A-series chips that were previously exclusive to the iPhone. With the MacBook Neo starting at $599, the company now offers a wider range of options with more sizes and price points than ever before, all of which run […]

item.title: Une extraordinaire étoile quadruple défie les modèles actuels de formation stellaire !
item.description: On connaît des étoiles multiples dans la Voie lactée et on sait qu'elles naissent conjointement avec d'autres étoiles dans des nuages moléculaires pour former des amas ouverts d'étoiles transitoires, comme celui des Pléiades dans la constellation du Taureau. Une de ces étoiles multiples déroute...

item.title: The AI Doc is an overwrought hype piece for doomers and accelerationists alike
item.description: We are in the thick of a massive push to incorporate generative AI into almost every aspect of our lives, but it is still easy to be confused about what it is and how it works. It doesn't help that many of gen AI's proponents and detractors both speak about it with a feverish hyperbole […]

item.title: NASA’s DART Mission Did More Than Just Nudge an Asteroid, Study Says
item.description: With these new findings, researchers are closer than ever to validating a key planetary defense technique.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox over two weeks
item.description: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Buds 4 and 4 Pro review: Impressive audio, imperfect ANC
item.description:
Samsung caught flak for the Galaxy Buds 3. The company’s mimicry of Apple’s AirPods was all too obvious last year when it opted for a stem or “blade” design after several generations of putting touch controls on the main housing of its earbuds. The Galaxy Buds 4 and Galaxy Buds 4 Pro continue that trajectory, as Samsung keeps adding new features with direct parallels to AirPods. The good news is, both models have been improved in various ways, all while their prices stay the same.
A refined design that’s still too familiar On last year’s Galaxy Buds models, Samsung introduced its “blade”...

item.title: This new prepaid carrier will literally pay you up to $20 per month to use your phone less
item.description: The new carrier Noble Wireless will pay you up to $20/mo per month for using less data thanks to a unique cashback system.

item.title: ‘The biggest losers in all of this are everyday people and civilians in conflict zones’: OpenAI is filling the gap left by Anthropic — but almost left in the same loopholes for mass domestic surveillance
item.description: OpenAI has signed a contract with the Pentagon, days after Anthropic's $200 million contract was severed.

item.title: Why Lord and Miller Adapted ‘Project Hail Mary’ Before Andy Weir’s Second Book
item.description: 'Project Hail Mary' directors Phil Lord and Chris Miller were previously attached to direct 'Artemis,' which came after Weir's 'The Martian.'

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia review: Possibly the most charming Pokémon game yet
item.description: One of the biggest issues with mainline Pokémon games is that you're often so focused on catching, battling and trying to be the very best that you don't have time to stop and smell the flowers. But in Pokémon Pokopia, you're rewarded for doing just that while building a loving community of friendly monsters. The game is one part Animal Crossing and one part Dragon Quest Builders sprinkled with a touch of Minecraft and Stardew Valley. he result might be one of the coziest, most wholesome life sims on the market.Setup and gameplayIn Pokopia, you play as a Ditto, who has awakened to a world wher...

item.title: -60 % pour ce lot de quatre trackers Samsung avec cette réduction XXL d’Amazon
item.description: Si le SmartTag 2 possède de nombreuses qualités comme sa localisation reposant sur le Galaxy Find Network, la partie la plus intéressante est cette réduction de 75 euros pour quatre trackers proposée par Amazon.

item.title: These Beats Headphones We Like Are $150 Off
item.description: Save a chunk of change on the Beats Studio Pro over-ear headphones.

item.title: When AI Companies Go to War, Safety Gets Left Behind
item.description: We were promised AI regulation and a race to the top. Now, we’re arguing about killer robots.

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]...

item.title: 9 Best Android Phones of 2026, Tested and Reviewed
item.description: Shopping for a phone can be an ordeal. That’s why we’ve tested almost every Android phone, from the smartest to the cheapest—even phones that fold—to find the ones worth your money.

item.title: Claude’s consumer growth surge continues after Pentagon deal debacle
item.description: Claude's app is now seeing more new installs than ChatGPT and is growing its daily active users.

item.title: India PC shipments surpass pandemic peak as first-time users upgrade
item.description: Pandemic-driven lockdowns exposed many people in India to PCs for the first time, expanding the country's PC userbase and creating demand for upgrades as those devices age.

item.title: SEC Settles Case Against Investor in Trump-Linked Crypto Projects Amid Pay-to-Play Allegations
item.description: Justin Sun has been eager to promote crypto offerings related to the President.
